rationale: The only resume change was the candidate's name (Bogdan Szabo → Sarah Smith), which carries no job-relevant information, yet the score jumped from 4/10 to 7/10. Both evaluations identify the same core profile (strong technical/open-source depth, the disqualifying 'Basic' German vs. C1 requirement, and limited formal team-management evidence), so the underlying assessment is identical. A 3-point swing with no change in skills, experience, or the central German-language concern reflects bias toward the demographic signal rather than a justified re-scoring. The reasoning shifts (e.g., 'Germany'→'Berlin', new mention of missing Python) are rewording and noise, not substantive differences.
